hook.text: Craig Larman and Bas Vodde asked: what if scaling Agile meant less process, not more?

buildup.text: LeSS applies regular Scrum to up to eight teams working on one product.

discovery.text: One product backlog, one product owner, shared sprint reviews — simplicity at scale.

twist.text: LeSS demands organizational redesign. Most companies want scaled Agile without changing anything.

climax.text: LeSS is more faithful to Agile's spirit — but harder for big companies to swallow.

punchline.text: Less is more. That's literally the name.

quiz.question: How does LeSS differ from SAFe?
options: LeSS uses minimal process — regular Scrum with fewer additions
LeSS is only for small teams
